note: Ladder rung 1: IdleTTL, a NAIVE idle-time-expiry strawman. NOT Continuum/CacheTTL. Continuum's mechanism is gap-aware PROTECTION (keep KV alive through predicted tool-call gaps); naive idle-time eviction is its motivating counterexample, not its contribution. A faithful Continuum is hint-consuming and lands as its own rung once the hint interface is wired. This row is the mechanism lower bound: how badly a pure idle-time signal misleads on this workload.
label_discipline: In every table this appears in: 'IdleTTL (naive idle-time strawman)', never 'TTL (Continuum)'. Emailing the Continuum authors a table showing 'their' policy at 1648% would be the anti-pitch; this is not their policy.

config._ratio_note: relabeled to cap/T; denominator = measured distinct tokens T=3,698,880 (11-session, block256); earlier 0.67 used a looser ~1.835M denominator; run config unchanged

mechanism_chain: Append-only agentic prefixes are re-read in full each turn, so an idle block is typically re-accessed on the next turn: idle != dead. The semantic-ephemeral analysis (docs/trace-conversion.md open finding) predicted this; here a policy family pays for it measurably (60 to 1500 points). This is the benchmark's job: converting a workload observation into a policy-relevant number with a mechanism attached.
converges_to_degenerate: IdleTTL's one-parameter sweep improves monotonically as TTL grows, and its optimum is the limit TTL -> infinity, which is exactly LRU (the mechanism turned off). A single-signal policy whose best case is its degenerate case is the cleanest possible evidence that the signal (idle time) is WRONG for the workload, not merely mistuned. This generalizes: it is the diagnostic to apply to every single-signal policy the ladder lands.
confirms_vs_tests: The data CONFIRMS Continuum's premise (idle-time signals mislead catastrophically on agentic re-reads). It does NOT test Continuum's contribution (gap-aware protection), which has not been run yet and could perform materially better.
